title: Fixing a React Memoized Component Re-renders on State Change

statement.markdown: A React component uses the `useMemo` hook to memoize its state, but it still re-renders unnecessarily when the state changes. Fix the code to prevent unnecessary re-renders.
statement.inputFormat: The component's state is an object with two properties: `count` and `person`. The `count` property is an integer, and the `person` property is an object with `name` and `age` properties.
statement.outputFormat: The component should not re-render when the state changes unless the `count` property changes.
statement.notes: You can use the `useEffect` hook to detect changes to the state, but you should only re-render the component when necessary.

examples.explanation: The component should not re-render when the state changes unless the `count` property changes.
The component should not re-render when the state changes unless the `count` property changes.

hints.text: Use the `useMemo` hook to memoize the state.
Use the `useEffect` hook to detect changes to the state.
Only re-render the component when necessary.

editorial.title: Fixing the React Component Re-renders
editorial.intuition: The `useMemo` hook is used to memoize the state, but it still re-renders unnecessarily when the state changes. We need to use the `useEffect` hook to detect changes to the state and only re-render the component when necessary.
editorial.bruteForce: We can use the `useMemo` hook to memoize the state and then use the `useEffect` hook to detect changes to the state. However, this approach is not efficient and can lead to unnecessary re-renders.
editorial.betterSolution: We can use a combination of the `useMemo` and `useEffect` hooks to memoize the state and detect changes to the state. This approach is more efficient and can prevent unnecessary re-renders.
editorial.optimalSolution: We can use a custom memoization function to memoize the state and detect changes to the state. This approach is the most efficient and can prevent unnecessary re-renders.
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: The custom memoization function correctly memoizes the state and detects changes to the state, preventing unnecessary re-renders.
commonMistakes: Not using the `useEffect` hook to detect changes to the state
